fuzzy_prd: We need a fluent guard-clause library for validating method inputs. The idea is simple: at the top of any method, you write one readable expression that either lets the value through unchanged or throws a well-described exception. Right now everyone hand-writes their own if-then-throw patterns, leading to different exception types, inconsistent messages, and missing the parameter name in the error. The library should cover: null rejection, string checks (empty, whitespace, length limits, equality, substrings, prefix/suffix), numeric ordering and range checks, boolean state checks, collection emptiness and size constraints, timestamp kind validation, enum membership, URI scheme and kind checks, and a custom condition guard for domain-specific rules. On rejection, the output always follows the same pattern: first a line naming the outcome, then the error category (we have four: null argument, out-of-range, general argument, and generic), then the parameter name, then a message explaining the problem. Range rejections add one more line echoing the actual offending value — follow the same convention our payment-range validator uses. On pass-through, return the original value rendered deterministically. Boolean values render with an initial capital. Collections render as bracketed comma-separated lists. Custom exception support: users should be able to raise their own exception type with a custom message by providing a condition and a factory.

oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: JSON object with op and operation-specific fields) -> stdout key=value lines for guard result or normalized error

trigger_keywords: what are the four error categories
what values can the error= field have
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The four error categories are: argument_null (null reference), argument_out_of_range (value outside range), argument (any other invalid argument), generic (fully custom failure). Range failures (argument_out_of_range) add an extra 'actual=<value>' line.

trigger_keywords: how do booleans render on pass-through
how is true or false displayed when pass-through
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Boolean values render with initial capital: 'True' or 'False' (not 'true'/'false'). Output: outcome=ok\nvalue=True or value=False.

trigger_keywords: what is the default comparison mode for string_reject_contains
what comparison is used when no comparison field is provided
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The default comparison mode is 'Ordinal'. When no 'comparison' field is supplied, the message includes '(comparison type: \'Ordinal\')'. The comparison name always appears in the contains/substring messages.

trigger_keywords: how does null render on pass-through
what does value= look like when passing null
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: A null reference renders as '<null>' (with angle brackets). Output: outcome=ok\nvalue=<null>

trigger_keywords: how does a URI render on pass-through for absolute URIs
how is an absolute URI rendered
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Absolute URIs render in their canonical form: scheme, host, normalized path. An authority-only URL (e.g. 'https://example.com') gains a trailing slash: 'https://example.com/'. Relative URIs render as the original string.

trigger_keywords: what does require_equals ignore_case comparison type show
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: string_require_equals_ignore_case uses comparison type 'OrdinalIgnoreCase' in the message: "String should be equal to '<other>' (comparison type: 'OrdinalIgnoreCase')."

The black-box adapter reads one JSON object from stdin and routes by the top-level 'op' field. The op vocabulary is exactly the operation names in public_test_cases, covering null, string, number, boolean, condition, collection, datetime, default, enum, URI, default-message, and custom-exception guard scenarios. Preserve each public case's exact normalized stdout and error message strings.

Exact pass-through rendering and exception messages are part of the test contract. Use public_test_cases as the authoritative list of op names, input fields, error categories, param names, actual_value fields, and message text. Do not substitute a generic fluent API-only contract for the JSON op dispatcher.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
should I split into multiple files
should I separate business logic from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: can I see the test inputs
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the same convention our payment-range validator uses
context_pointers.actual_meaning: argument_out_of_range errors include an extra 'actual=<value>' line at the end, echoing the offending value back to the caller.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have specific information about that constraint. Please check start.md or the test cases in rcb_tests/public_test_cases/.

feature10_1_uri_http_scheme.json.description: HTTP/HTTPS scheme: reject (or require) the http scheme, and reject (or require) the https scheme; the absolute URI is returned unchanged when it passes.

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Uri scheme should not be http.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Uri scheme should be http.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Uri scheme should not be https.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Uri scheme should be https.

feature10_2_uri_scheme.json.description: Named scheme: reject a URI whose scheme equals a given scheme name, or require that exact scheme (rejecting any other); the scheme name appears in the error.

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Uri scheme should not be ftp.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Uri scheme should be https.

feature10_3_uri_kind.json.description: Absolute vs relative: reject an absolute URI, require an absolute URI (rejecting relative), reject a relative URI, and require a relative URI (rejecting absolute).

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Uri should be relative.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Uri should be absolute.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Uri should be absolute.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Uri should be relative.

feature10_4_uri_port.json.description: URI port: reject a URI served on a given port number, or require that exact port (rejecting any other); the port number appears in the error.

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Uri port should not be 8080.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Uri port should be 80.

feature11_1_default_messages.json.description: Default error messages: the three low-level error signals (null-argument, argument, and out-of-range) produce their standard messages, with the parameter name reported in its own field and, for out-of-range, the offending value reported on its own line.

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument_null
param=paramName
message=Value cannot be null.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=paramName
message=Value does not fall within the expected range.

outcome=threw
error=argument_out_of_range
param=paramName
message=Specified argument was out of the range of valid values.
actual=42

feature11_2_custom_exceptions.json.description: Customized errors: an error signal may carry a custom message that replaces the default text, or a caller-supplied factory that produces an entirely different exception type (optionally receiving the parameter name).

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument
param=paramName
message=custom message

outcome=threw
error=generic
param=
message=totally custom

outcome=threw
error=generic
param=
message=param: paramName

feature1_null_rejection.json.description: Reject a null reference: when the supplied value is null the guard raises a null-argument error naming the parameter; a non-null value passes through unchanged.

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument_null
param=value
message=Value cannot be null.

outcome=ok
value=hello

feature2_1_string_blank.json.description: Reject blank text: an empty string or a string made up only of white space is rejected; any string with visible content passes through.

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should not be empty.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should not be white space only.

feature2_2_string_length.json.description: Constrain text length: reject strings longer than, shorter than, exactly equal to, or not equal to a given character count; otherwise pass through.

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should not be longer than 3 characters.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should not be shorter than 3 characters.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String length should not be equal to 5.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String length should be equal to 5.

feature2_3_string_equality.json.description: Compare text for equality: reject equality (case sensitive or ignoring case), require equality, and apply an explicit comparison mode whose name appears in the error.

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should not be equal to 'abc'.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should not be equal to 'abc' (case insensitive).

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should be equal to 'xyz' (comparison type: 'Ordinal').

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should be equal to 'xyz' (comparison type: 'OrdinalIgnoreCase').

feature2_4_string_substring.json.description: Match text fragments: reject or require a substring (optionally under a named comparison mode), and reject or require a given prefix or suffix.

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should not contain 'world' (comparison type: 'Ordinal').

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should not contain 'WORLD' (comparison type: 'OrdinalIgnoreCase').

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should contain 'xyz' (comparison type: 'Ordinal').

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should not start with 'hello'.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should start with 'bye'.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should not end with '.txt'.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=String should end with '.md'.

feature3_1_number_ordering.json.description: Order comparisons: reject a value greater than, or less than, a threshold; range errors echo the offending actual value.

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument_out_of_range
param=value
message=Value should not be greater than 4.
actual=5

outcome=threw
error=argument_out_of_range
param=value
message=Value should not be less than 4.
actual=3

feature3_2_number_equality.json.description: Numeric equality: reject a value equal to a target, or require a value equal to a target; otherwise pass through.

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Value should not be equal to 4.

outcome=threw
error=argument
param=value
message=Value should be equal to 4.

feature3_3_number_sign.json.description: Sign checks: reject a positive value or reject a negative value; the complementary sign (including zero where applicable) passes through.

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument_out_of_range
param=value
message=Value should not be greater than 0.
actual=5

outcome=threw
error=argument_out_of_range
param=value
message=Value should not be less than 0.
actual=-5

feature3_4_number_range.json.description: Inclusive range: reject a value outside an inclusive [min, max] interval; the range error echoes the actual value.

cases.expected_output:
outcome=threw
error=argument_out_of_range
param=value
message=Value should be between 0 and 4.
actual=5

error=argument
param=value
